title: castilleja affinis subsp. neglecta
intro: Castilleja affinis subsp. neglecta, also known as Mountain Indian Paintbrush, is a flowering plant species in the borer family (Orobanchaceae) that is native to western North America. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, due to the presence of alkaloids and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of Castilleja affinis subsp. neglecta include its potential to alleviate respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its ability to soothe skin irritations and wounds. Herbal preparations of Castilleja affinis subsp. neglecta can be made into teas, tinctures, or salves, and are often used in traditional medicine to promote overall well-being.
